Journal.Title: The Journal of innovations in cardiac rhythm management

Article.ArticleTitle: Heart Rate Threshold Settings for Ventricular Tachycardia in Patients with Wearable Cardioverter-defibrillators.

Abstract.AbstractText: The wearable cardioverter-defibrillator (WCD) uses programmable heart rate (HR) and detection time to determine the need for shock delivery, but the optimal ventricular tachycardia (VT) HR detection zone is unknown. The LifeVest™ (ZOLL Medical, Chelmsford, MA, USA) WCD has a default VT detection rate of 150 bpm. We found that 15% of appropriate shocks among WCD recipients were delivered for VT 150-170 bpm and that 30% of these patients experienced loss of consciousness. Based on these findings, a programmed VT detection threshold of 150 bpm should be considered.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright: © 2026 Innovations in Cardiac Rhythm Management.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center, Boston, MA, USA.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Mr. Ringquist and Ms. Bianco are employed by ZOLL Medical and were involved in the development of the topic and statistical analysis. Dr. Tung reports non-financial support from ZOLL Medical, during the conduct of the study. No funding information was provided.

Reference.Citation: Chung MK, Szymkiewicz SJ, Shao M, et al. Aggregate national experience with the wearable cardioverter-defibrillator: event rates, compliance, and survival. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2010;56(3):194–203. doi: 10.1016/j.jacc.2010.04.016.
